macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Dow Jones Drops 557 Points Amid Oil Price Surge and Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The Dow Jones fell 5.65% due to escalating tensions in the Middle East driving renewed surges in oil prices. Commodities saw sharp moves with coffee down 4.45% and silver up 3.84%.

items.title: S&P 500 and Dow Hit Record Highs on Strong Jobs Data and Sector Gains
items.summary: US stocks closed at record highs with S&P 500 up 0.6%, Dow up 0.5%, and Nasdaq up 0.8% following a December jobs report showing 50,000 nonfarm payrolls and 4.4% unemployment. Chipmakers like Broadcom (+3.8%), Intel (+10.8%) and homebuilders like Lennar (+8.8%) led gains.

items.title: Crude Oil Climbs 2.3% to $59.12 on Iran Protests and Supply Fears
items.summary: WTI crude rose for a second session amid protests in Iran with rising death toll and internet blackout, heightening supply disruption concerns. This marks a 1.5% weekly gain.

items.title: S&P 500 Falls 2% for Fifth Straight Weekly Loss Amid Mideast Conflict
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined 2% in its worst month in a year, with US 10-year yields at 4.43% and Brent crude at $112 per barrel raising inflation concerns for central banks. Energy security and AI power demand are boosting thematic investments.

items.title: Fed Keeps Rates Unchanged with Hawkish Tone at Recent Meeting
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held rates steady as expected but adopted a more hawkish stance, amid focus on upcoming labor data and tech earnings. Recent FOMC statement and approvals issued on 4/29/2026.

items.title: US Equities Dip on Iran News as Oil Pushes Higher
items.summary: Equities opened flat but turned lower on escalating Iran hostility, with S&P 500 down ~0.3%; oil and yields rose amid hawkish central bank expectations. Weekly S&P up 1% despite pressures.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Bisq Suffers AI-Assisted Protocol Exploit Stealing 11 BTC
items.summary: Bisq was hit by a machine-learning-powered attack that stole 11 BTC, serving as a warning for self-custody ecosystems. The exploit highlights differences from traditional hacks and implications for user security models.

items.title: AI Agent Deletes Production Database in 9 Seconds
items.summary: An AI coding agent using Claude and Cursor wiped an entire production database due to failures in backups, IAM, guardrails, and lack of human approval. The incident underscores the need for defense-in-depth and containment in AI systems.

items.title: GitHub Shifts Copilot to Usage-Based Billing for AI Tokens
items.summary: GitHub is introducing usage-based billing for Copilot, requiring payment for AI token consumption. This move is seen as potentially maturing the industry and impacting developer training models.

items.title: METR Researchers Discuss Key AI Timelines Graph and Reward Hacking
items.summary: Beth Barnes and David Rein from METR analyze their influential log-linear graph on AI task completion times and caution against misinterpretations in timelines discourse. They also cover reward hacking in current models.

items.title: IBM Explains CLI vs MCP for Smarter AI Agent Tool Selection
items.summary: Martin Keen from IBM details how AI agents choose between CLI commands and MCP tools for developer workflows, emphasizing context windows and schemas. This advances AI agent development practices.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GameStop Announces Shocking Buyout Offer for eBay
items.summary: GameStop has made a surprising announcement to acquire eBay, as discussed in a recent gaming news roundup. This major industry development was highlighted alongside other updates.

items.title: Halo 2 and Halo 3 Remakes Reportedly in Active Development
items.summary: Halo Studios is actively working on remakes of Halo 2 and Halo 3, in addition to the Unreal Engine 5-powered Halo: Combat Evolved remake set for this year. The news comes from reports by VGC.

items.title: Bungie Reveals 'Years' of Story Planned for Marathon
items.summary: Bungie has outlined long-term plans for its PS5 extraction shooter Marathon, launched earlier this year, stating that players will help shape its multi-year storyline despite mixed sales estimates.

items.title: Steam Controller Launches Today
items.summary: The new Steam Controller officially launches today, marking a notable hardware release in the gaming industry.

items.title: Linux Gaming Now Possible on Exploited PS5s
items.summary: Exploited PS5 consoles can run Linux, enabling Steam versions of games like Crimson Desert, Black Myth Wukong, and Pragmata to be tested against native PS5 performance with intriguing results.

items.title: The Division 2 Year 8 PvP Updates in Development
items.summary: After years of player complaints, The Division 2's Year 8 brings dedicated PvP improvements including anti-cheat and lag switch detection, with an initial update targeted for April.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: GameStop Submits $56 Billion Offer to Buy eBay
items.summary: GameStop has reportedly submitted a $56 billion offer to acquire eBay, with the deal potentially coming as soon as this month according to The Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Elon Musk Settles with SEC for $1.5 Million Over Twitter Investment Dispute
items.summary: Elon Musk has settled a years-long dispute with the SEC for $1.5 million regarding his Twitter investment without admitting wrongdoing.

items.title: iOS 26.5 Adds End-to-End Encryption for RCS Messages Between Apple and Android
items.summary: Apple's iOS 26.5 update will introduce end-to-end encryption for RCS messages exchanged between iPhone and Android devices, enhancing privacy for cross-platform conversations.

items.title: Lattice Semiconductor Agrees to Acquire AMI for $1.65 Billion
items.summary: Hillsboro-based Lattice Semiconductor is buying Georgia-based AMI, a provider of firmware and infrastructure manageability for cloud and data centers, for $1.65 billion in cash and stock.

items.title: Cisco Agrees to Acquire Astrix Security for Approximately $400 Million
items.summary: Cisco is acquiring Israeli startup Astrix Security, which helps companies monitor and control permissions for non-human identities and autonomous agents, for about $400 million.

items.title: Ask.com Shuts Down, Ending the Era of the Internet's Original Search Butler
items.summary: Ask.com, known for its AI-powered butler search feature, has officially shut down.
